name: Paella (New York Times Cook Book)
recipeIngredient: 2 ounces Ham, cut into thin strips
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove meat from the lobster.
2. Shell and devein shrimp.
3. Scrub mussels and clams clean.
4. Cut chicken into medium-sized serving pieces.
5. Combine oregano, peppercorns, garlic, salt, 2 tablespoons olive oil, and vinegar in a bowl. Mash with the back of a spoon or using a mortar until well combined.
6. Heat remaining olive oil in a deep, heavy skillet over moderate heat. Brown chicken lightly, about 3-4 minutes per side.
7. Add ham, chorizo, salt pork, onion, green pepper, coriander, and capers to the pan. Cook for 10 minutes over low heat, stirring occasionally.
8. Stir in tomato sauce and rice, then cook for an additional 5 minutes.
9. Add boiling water, saffron, and shrimp to the mixture. Mix well and place a lid on the skillet.
10. Simmer rapidly until the liquid is absorbed, about 20 minutes.
11. Use a large spoon to mix the rice from top to bottom.
12. Stir in lobster meat, peas, and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
13. Steam mussels and clams in a little water until their shells open. Heat pimientos and drain.
14. Garnish with the steamed mussels and clams, as well as pimientos.
Yield: 6-8 servings
